## [00:00 - 03:00]

Okay, I'm going to give you in this segment the first rule of selling. This is critical, absolutely essential to understand this. The first rule of selling, no matter what product you are representing, always, always, always agree with the customer. Always. This is the single most important, most basic, and most commonly violated rule in all of selling. In fact, in all of communication. If you want agreement, you have to be agreeable. Okay? ## [03:00 - 06:05]

The salesperson who wants agreement must give agreement to the customer before agreement can ever be achieved. Even when a buyer is making ridiculous, crazy claims, or even exaggerations, agree. I'm with you, sir. I agree with you, sir. I understand, sir. Just because you think what he's saying is ridiculous doesn't mean he thinks it's ridiculous. If he thinks something's black and you think it's white, hey, you're both right. Now I know you want to take your belief system and shove it down his throat, but the truth is it's black to him. If he thinks it's black and you want to get that sale, you need to see from his viewpoint how it could be black. If he thinks he should wait and think about it and you disagree, he'll solidify. He'll become difficult. His objection will become valid and he will wait and you will never get him to the close. Now I'm giving you something very basic. ## [06:05 - 06:09]
